dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: Hey Sam! I got your postcard yesterday. You went to Spain?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: Yes! It was amazing. The weather was perfect, the food was delicious, and the people were so friendly.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: That sounds wonderful. Did you speak Spanish with them?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: I tried, but I only know a few words. I really want to learn more. Will you learn Spanish with me?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: Spanish? That's a great idea! When do you want to begin?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: There's a class that starts next Monday. We can go together every week.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: Perfect! We will do it. I bet we can learn quickly if we support each other.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: Exactly! And we can practice together too. This is going to be fun.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: Sam, can we talk? I'm very tired today.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: What's wrong? You look worried.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: It's the Spanish homework. I have so much to do every day. The teacher gives us three exercises, and I write for hours.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: I agree, it's a lot. But we already paid for the class...

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: I know, but I don't have time anymore. I work, I study, and now this. I wonder if I can continue.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: But Alex, we said we will do it together. You can't stop now. We're learning so much!

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: Are we? I sing the songs, I say the words, but I don't feel like I'm really learning.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: Give it more time. It's only been one month. Learning a language is difficult at the beginning.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: Alex! I have an idea. Why don't we change how we study?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: We can reduce the homework time. Instead of writing everything, let's just speak together for thirty minutes every day.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: Speak in Spanish? About what?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: About anything! The weather, our day, what we did yesterday. It will be more fun and less like work.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: That's actually a good idea. I hate doing so much writing. Speaking is more useful anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: Exactly! And next year, we can go to Spain together and use our Spanish there.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: Okay, I agree. Let's do it your way. Thanks for the support, my friend.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: That's what friends are for! Now, let's practice. How do you say 'I am happy' in Spanish?
